title: Connecting to a MUD
description: Learn about host/port configuration, SSL options, and connection management

sections.title: Understanding Host and Port
sections.content:
MUDs (Multi-User Dungeons) are accessed via TCP/IP connections. Each MUD server has:

**Host** - The domain name or IP address of the server (e.g., 'darkrealms.org' or '192.168.1.100')

**Port** - A number that identifies the specific service on that server. Common MUD ports include:
- 23 (traditional Telnet)
- 2700-2702 (common game ports)
- 8080 (HTTP-based games)
- 5555 (many custom servers)

Check your MUD's connection instructions for the correct host and port.

sections.title: SSL/TLS Connections
sections.content:
Some MUDs offer secure connections using SSL/TLS encryption. To use SSL:

1. Enable SSL in the connection settings
2. Some games use a separate port for SSL (common: port + 1 or port + 1000)

SSL protects your login credentials and game data from eavesdropping on public networks.

sections.title: Quick Connect vs Saved Connections
sections.content:
**Quick Connect** is ideal for:
- One-time connections
- Testing new MUDs
- Situations where you don't need to save settings

**Saved Connections** are better for:
- Regular play on the same MUD
- Storing automation (aliases, triggers, variables)
- Multiple characters on the same server

Saved connections store your profile including automation rules.

sections.title: Connection Timeout
sections.content:
If a connection times out:

1. Verify the host and port are correct
2. Check your internet connection
3. The MUD server may be down - try again later
4. Some firewalls block MUD ports - check your network settings

The default connection timeout is 30 seconds.

sections.title: Reconnection
sections.content:
If your connection drops:

1. The client will attempt to reconnect automatically
2. Your session state (aliases, triggers) is preserved
3. You may need to re-authenticate with the MUD server

Check the Troubleshooting section if you experience frequent disconnections.
